zh.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymour於1622年出生在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯。她於1642年9月12日與Richard Yorke在英國諾薩普頓史爾笛卡爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1688年在英國巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼享年66歲，埋葬在英國巴金哈姆爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymour于1622年出生在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯。她于1642年9月12日与Richard Yorke在英国諾薩普頓史爾笛卡爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1688年在英国巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼享年66岁，埋葬在英国巴金哈姆爾。

nameMeanings.name: Seymour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), and possibly also from Saint-Maur-des-Bois (Manche) and Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine), named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Maur (see   Moore   3). The family of this name who have held the title Duke of Somerset since 1547 are said by some to have originated in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), while others claim they are from Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine). They rose to prominence under the Tudors, when Edward Seymour (died 1552) held a high position of office under Henry VIII, who was married to Edward's sister, Jane. The Norman name has been established in Ireland since at least the 17th century.

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Semar
meanings: Germanized form of French Saint Marc, the name of a Huguenot family that migrated to the Palatinate.
English: variant of   Seymour  . This surname is very rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymour was born in 1622, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England. She married Richard Yorke on 12 September 1642, in Old, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1688, in Olney, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 66, and was buried in Buckinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymour est née en 1622, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Richard Yorke le 12 septembre 1642, à Old, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1688, à Olney, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymour wurde 1622 in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England geboren. Sie hat am 12. September 1642 in Old, Northamptonshire, England Richard Yorke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1688 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Olney, Buckinghamshire, England und wurde in Buckinghamshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymour è nata il nel 1622 nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England. Lei sposato Richard Yorke il 12 settembre 1642, nel Old, Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1688, nel Olney, Buckinghamshire, England, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolta a Buckinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymourは1622年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスで生まれた。1642年9月12日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアオルドでRichard Yorkeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1688年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアオルニーで66歳で死去し，イギリスブクインガムシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymour(은)는 1622년 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1642년 9월 12일 영국 노르섬프토실르 드에서 Richard Yorke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1688년 영국 벅잉함실르 니에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 벅잉함실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymour nasceu em 1622, em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Richard Yorke em 12 de setembro de 1642, em Old, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1688, em Olney, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 66 anos, e foi sepultada em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymour родилась в 1622 г. в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Yorke 12 сентября 1642 г. в местности олд, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1688 г. в местности олни, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности буккингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Mary Jane Seymour nació en 1622, en Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Richard Yorke el 12 de septiembre de 1642, en Old, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1688, en Olney, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultada en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.
